summary.intent: Modernize the Moltis website by adding a visual 'Works with your stack' section showing supported AI providers/LLMs with icons, inspired by Polyphony's pill design, and update the hero messaging to reflect current features.
summary.outcome: Added a glassmorphism pill section with 4 categorized rows (Cloud Providers, Local & BYOM, OAuth, Channels), sourced official brand SVGs/raster logos for 23 icons, extracted all CSS to style.css, updated hero title/meta tags, and committed + pushed the changes as a PR.

repo: Website lives in website/ subdirectory within the worktree; only index.en.html exists — other locale files need separate propagation
The site uses a Tailwind-based dark mode via .dark class on the root element
Home page container was max-w-2xl; widened to max-w-3xl to accommodate the pills section

code.finding: Extracted from inline <style> block; pill-icon class uses filter: invert(1) for dark mode on SVG icons; pill-icon-color class added for raster logos (LM Studio webp, MiniMax png) that should not be inverted
Pills section placed after value proposition cards, not between screenshot gallery and cards as originally planned — position was moved in a later iteration

workflow: Simple Icons (simpleicons.org) is the canonical source for brand SVGs at small sizes
Raster logos (webp/png) need a separate CSS class to opt out of dark-mode invert filter
Inline SVGs should use currentColor fill so dark mode invert filter works correctly; hardcoded hex colors break this
Extracting SVGs to individual files (images/icons/) makes broken icons easier to identify and replace independently

friction: Teams icon went through 3 iterations before landing on the official Simple Icons version — initial attempts were a rough 'T' glyph and a people-silhouette that both rendered poorly at 16px
DeepSeek icon was initially an incomplete heart shape; replaced twice before settling on official whale logo
Cerebras icon required multiple attempts — initial 'C' lettermark felt broken, needed proper concentric arc design
CSS extraction was done as a reactive step after user request, not proactively
Rate limit hit mid-session during the icon sourcing phase, blocking progress until reset
open_items: Changes are only in index.en.html — other locale files need the same updates propagated
LM Studio has no public SVG; using .webp raster logo which may look blurry on retina if not sized correctly
MiniMax, Z.AI, and Venice have no widely recognized icons — MiniMax uses a raster PNG, Z.AI has a hand-crafted lettermark, Venice has no icon
Scroll-reveal JS is inline in index.en.html — could be extracted to a JS file for consistency with the CSS extraction
